revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Izusan Shrine]][[fa:معبد ایزوسان]][[fr:Izusan-jinja]][[ja:伊豆山神社]][[zh:伊豆山神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan}} {{No footnotes|date=June 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Izusan Jinja
伊豆山神社 | image = File:231007 Izusan-jinja Atami Japan02s3.jpg | caption = ''Honden'' of Izusan Jinja | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.115455|N|139.082558|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ninigi|qid=Q1056017|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Oshihomimi|qid=Q462555|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}; {{ill|Takuhata Chijihime no Mikoto|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 708-1 Uenochi, Izusan, Atami, Shizuoka | website = {{Official website|1=http://izusanjinjya.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Izusan|qid=Q97201808|1=Izusan}} }} {{nihongo|'''Izusan Jinja'''|伊豆山神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the city of [[:en:Atami, Shizuoka|Atami]] in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine has been known by many names in its long history, including {{nihongo|Soto Jinja|走湯神社}}. The shrine’s main festival is held annually on April 15. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Izusan Jinja is the {{nihongo|Izu Dai[[gongen]]|伊豆大権現}}, an amalgamation of {{nihongo|[[Kagu-tsuchi|Honomusuhi-no-mikoto]]| 火牟須比命}}, {{nihongo|[[Izanagi]]|伊邪那伎命}} and his consort {{nihongo|{{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}|伊邪那美命|}} ==History== The {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=volcanic springs|1=Onsen}} and a {{ill|Geyser|en|Geyser|qid=Q83471|lt=Geyser|1=Geyser}} at Atami were regarded in ancient Japan as manifestations of the [[kami]], and [[fudoki|ancient records]] indicate that a shrine already existed here by 594 AD. The actual date of the shrine’s foundation is unknown, with shrine legend mentioning the semi-legendary {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|qid=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}} and {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} as possible candidates. The shrine legend also claims that it was appointed as an official shrine for prayers to the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|Imperial clan]] under {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, {{ill|Emperor Seinei|en|Emperor Seinei|qid=Q357273|lt=Emperor Seinei|1=Emperor Seinei}}, [[Emperor Bidatsu]], [[Emperor Kōtoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Go-Nara|en|Emperor Go-Nara|qid=Q349467|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|1=Emperor Go-Nara}}. The shrine later became a center of the [[Shugendō]] mountain cult. After he was exiled to Izu, [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] worshipped at Izusan Shrine for divine assistance in his struggle to overthrow the [[:en:Heike clan|Heike clan]] in the {{ill|Genpei War|en|Genpei War|qid=Q737542|lt=Genpei War|1=Genpei War}}. He also used the shrine grounds as a location to rally the local ''[[gōzoku]]'' clans to his side. Izu Shrine is also noted as the location to which Yoritomo and {{ill|Hōjō Masako|en|Hōjō Masako|qid=Q463961|lt=Hōjō Masako|1=Hōjō Masako}} eloped – an event which brought the {{ill|Hōjō clan|en|Hōjō clan|qid=Q834364|lt=Hōjō clan|1=Hōjō clan}} to his side and which was instrumental in the successful establishment of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|qid=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}. Yoritomo and Masako later had the shrine rebuilt on a large scale, and its holdings extended as far as {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|qid=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province|1=Echigo Province}}. The shrine was later patronized by the {{ill|Later Hōjō clan|en|Later Hōjō clan|qid=Q431928|lt=Odawara Hōjō|1=Later Hōjō clan}}, the [[Imagawa clan]] and the [[Tokugawa clan]]. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, visitors to Atami increased due to its location near the {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|qid=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (road)}} connecting {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|qid=Q215646|lt=Edo|1=Edo}} with {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, and the need for ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]s'' to make regular journeys because of the ''[[sankin-kōtai]]'' system. The shrine was granted revenues of 300 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|qid=Q855048|lt=Koku|1=Koku}}'' by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. Most of the shrine records and many of its structures were lost in the destruction of the ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement following the [[Meiji Restoration]]. Crown Prince {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|qid=Q34479|lt=Hirohito|1=Hirohito}} visited the shrine in 1914, and a [[:en:Japanese Black Pine|Japanese Black Pine]] was planted next to the ''[[Honden]]'' to commemorate the visit. The shrine was granted the Rank of National Shrine, 3rd Rank ('''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Kokuhei Shōsha]]''') under {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} in 1928. ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} ==References== * Maas, Jeffrey P. ''Yoritomo and the Founding of the First Bakufu: The Origins of Dual Government in Japan.'' Stanford University Press. (1999). {{ISBN|0-8047-3591-3}} * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Izusan Shrine]] {{Commons category|Izusan-jinja}} [[fa:معبد ایزوسان]] [[fr:Izusan-jinja]] [[ja:伊豆山神社]] [[zh:伊豆山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q611460}} [[Category:Atami, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
